sanskrit: अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः । सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
hindiTranslation: जो आकाङ्क्षासे रहित, बाहरभीतरसे पवित्र, दक्ष, उदासीन, व्यथासे रहित और सभी आरम्भोंका अर्थात् नयेनये कर्मोंके आरम्भका सर्वथा त्यागी है, वह मेरा भक्त मुझे प्रिय है ॥
englishTranslation: One who has no expectation, is pure, skilled, detached, free from anxiety, and has given up all undertakings is dear to me, my devotee.
essence: True closeness releases craving, anxiety, and compulsive beginning.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says his beloved devotee has no craving, stays inwardly clean, acts skillfully, remains detached, and drops anxious new beginnings.
simpleInsight: Devotion here feels light, not needy. Love becomes cleaner when grasping and restless starting are gone.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen while Krishna defines the devotee who is truly close to him. This verse adds another layer to the chapter’s portrait: the beloved one has no craving, no inner agitation, and no urge to keep launching new projects from restlessness.
modernRelevance: You keep opening new tabs, new plans, new fixes because stillness feels unbearable. This verse points to a calmer strength: do what is needed, but stop feeding anxiety with constant beginnings.
translationLiteral: अनपेक्षः (without expectation) / शुचिः (pure) / दक्षः (skilled) / उदासीनः (detached) / गतव्यथः (free from distress) / सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी (one who has given up all undertakings) / यः (who) / मद्भक्तः (my devotee) / सः (that one) / मे (to me) / प्रियः (dear)
